rationale.R1.2: The artifact is a methods-and-results section; a generic 'check' on such text naturally includes verifying that the study design, statistical approach, and drawn conclusions are internally coherent — e.g., whether the mixed-effects model choice is justified by the design, whether counterbalancing claims follow from the described procedure.
rationale.R1.3: The section closes with empirical claims (numeric phrases elicit higher trust than qualitative ones; substantial inter-rater agreement on thematic coding). Checking whether those claims are grounded in what the described data and analysis can actually support is a central review task.
rationale.R1.6: The prose has observable tense inconsistencies ('each participant completes' vs past-tense surrounding text), run-on sentence constructions, and stylistic unevenness. Surface-level checking is a plausible primary intent of 'check this.'
